title: [Docs] Outdated default for --pull in 'podman build'
body:
### Issue Description

Documentation (https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-build.1.html#pull-policy) says that the default value for `--pull` is `always`. However, **if I don't** specify `--pull=always` it just uses the cache (it completes instantly) but **if I do** specify `--pull=always` it pauses for a second and actually checks if that's the newest (which can be confirmed by the logs saying: `Copying blob 38f5a258dd4f skipped: already exists`).

If `always` is the default policy, why do I have to specify `--pull=always` for it to work?

Related discussion: https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/20121

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. `$ less Containerfile`
   ```Dockerfile
   FROM archlinux:base
   ```
2. `$ podman build -f Containerfile`
3. `$ podman build -f Containerfile --pull=always`

### Describe the results you received

`--pull=always` does not seem to be the default value for `$ podman build`

### Describe the results you expected

Documentation should reflect the correct default value for `--pull`

comments.body:
I think `true` is not equal to `always`, `true` will only pull if image is not present on `local` while `always` will pull always. Atleast buildah is more clear on this one. https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/docs/buildah-build.1.md

Would you like to raise a PR to correct this ?
Let's check the code instead of the docs to be sure ;-)
